semaglutide.compounding: Removed from the FDA drug shortage list (semaglutide injection resolved Feb 2025). Compounding of 'essentially copies' of the approved drug is generally not permitted under 503A/503B now that the shortage has resolved — verify current FDA shortage status and state board rules before compounding.
semaglutide.offLabelNote: Use outside approved BMI/indication thresholds is off-label.

tirzepatide.compounding: Tirzepatide shortage declared resolved by FDA (2025); compounding of copies is generally restricted now that supply is restored. Verify current shortage status and state rules.

liraglutide.compounding: Commercially available — not a compounding candidate.

naltrexone-bupropion.compounding: Components are individually approved; compounded combinations exist but the fixed-dose product is commercially available.
naltrexone-bupropion.offLabelNote: Compounded ratios/doses are off-label relative to Contrave.

bpc-157.compounding: In 2023 the FDA placed BPC-157 in Category 2 of the 503A bulk substances list (significant safety risk / insufficient data), meaning it should not be compounded under 503A pending review. Availability is restricted; verify current FDA bulk-substance status and state board position.
bpc-157.offLabelNote: All clinical use is investigational/off-label; document informed consent.

sermorelin.status: Previously FDA-approved (Geref, withdrawn for commercial reasons, not safety); now compounded.
sermorelin.compounding: Compounded by 503A pharmacies. Regulatory scrutiny of GHRH/peptide compounding is increasing — verify the pharmacy's sourcing and current FDA position.
approvedUses: Historically: diagnostic of GH secretion; pediatric GH deficiency (product withdrawn).

cjc-1295-ipamorelin.compounding: Both components face FDA bulk-substance scrutiny; ipamorelin and CJC-1295 have been flagged in the agency's peptide review. Verify current 503A bulk-list status before compounding.

ipamorelin.compounding: Under FDA peptide bulk-substance review; restricted availability. Verify current status.

tesamorelin.compounding: Commercial product available; compounded use is off-label and faces peptide-compounding scrutiny.
approvedUses: Reduction of excess visceral abdominal fat in HIV-associated lipodystrophy.

thymosin-beta-4.status: NOT FDA-approved (TB-500 is a synthetic fragment). Investigational.
thymosin-beta-4.schedule: Not controlled (banned by WADA in sport).
thymosin-beta-4.compounding: Flagged in FDA peptide bulk-substance review; not an approved compounding substance. Verify current status.

testosterone-cypionate.compounding: Commercially available; compounded concentrations available via 503A. DEA controlled — follow controlled-substance prescribing rules.
testosterone-cypionate.offLabelNote: Use in women and 'age-related low T' without classic hypogonadism is off-label.

testosterone-cream.status: FDA-approved transdermal products exist (AndroGel, etc.); compounded creams common.
testosterone-cream.compounding: Compounded transdermal testosterone via 503A (esp. low-dose female and custom male concentrations).
testosterone-cream.offLabelNote: Female low-dose androgen therapy is off-label (ISSWSH supports for HSDD).

estradiol.compounding: Commercial bioidentical products available; compounded bioidentical hormone therapy (cBHT) widely used but the 2020 NASEM report questioned routine cBHT use where approved products exist.
estradiol.offLabelNote: Pellets and some compounded combinations are not FDA-approved.

progesterone.compounding: Approved oral micronized product available; compounded forms (creams, troches) used but cream is inadequate for endometrial protection.
progesterone.offLabelNote: Sleep/mood and transdermal use are off-label.

dhea.status: Sold as a dietary supplement (OTC); vaginal prasterone (Intrarosa) is FDA-approved.
dhea.schedule: Not controlled in the US (banned in some sports).
dhea.compounding: OTC supplement; compounded/Rx forms available. Quality of OTC products varies.
approvedUses: Intrarosa (prasterone) for moderate-to-severe dyspareunia due to GSM.

anastrozole.status: FDA-approved (Arimidex and generics) for breast cancer.
anastrozole.compounding: Commercial tablets available; compounded low-dose used in men's TRT (off-label).
approvedUses: Hormone-receptor-positive breast cancer (adjuvant and metastatic) in postmenopausal women.
anastrozole.offLabelNote: Use in men for estradiol control during TRT is off-label.

hcg.compounding: Compounded hCG used in TRT/fertility protocols. Note: hCG for weight loss is explicitly NOT FDA-approved and the agency has acted against hCG weight-loss claims.
approvedUses: Hypogonadotropic hypogonadism / induction of ovulation / prepubertal cryptorchidism.
hcg.offLabelNote: Fertility preservation during TRT is off-label; weight-loss use is prohibited/unapproved.
references: FDA statements on hCG weight-loss products

nad-plus.status: NOT an FDA-approved drug. Used as an injectable/IV compounded therapy or supplement precursor.
nad-plus.compounding: Injectable NAD+ is compounded via 503A/503B; NAD+ as an injectable has been discussed in FDA bulk-substance reviews. Verify current status.

metformin.offLabelNote: PCOS, prediabetes, and longevity/metabolic use are off-label (TAME trial investigating aging endpoints).

rapamycin.compounding: Commercial tablets/solution available; off-label intermittent low-dose longevity use prescribed from approved product.
approvedUses: Prophylaxis of organ rejection in renal transplant
rapamycin.offLabelNote: Longevity/healthspan use is off-label and investigational (PEARL and other trials ongoing).

berberine.offLabelNote: Metabolic use is supplement-based; significant CYP3A4/P-gp interaction profile.

nmn.status: Regulatory status in flux. FDA stated NMN is excluded from the dietary-supplement definition (was studied as a drug), creating uncertainty; enforcement/marketing status evolving.
nmn.compounding: Marketed as a supplement by many vendors despite FDA's position; verify current FDA stance.
nmn.offLabelNote: All use is supplement/investigational; regulatory status contested.

pt-141.status: FDA-approved as Vyleesi (bremelanotide) for premenopausal HSDD; compounded forms also used.
pt-141.compounding: Vyleesi commercially available; compounded PT-141 (incl. nasal) used off-label, under peptide-compounding scrutiny.
approvedUses: Hypoactive sexual desire disorder (HSDD) in premenopausal women (Vyleesi).
pt-141.offLabelNote: Use in men and compounded routes are off-label.

oxytocin.status: FDA-approved (Pitocin) as an injectable for obstetric use.
oxytocin.compounding: Compounded sublingual/intranasal oxytocin used off-label for wellness; not FDA-approved by those routes.
approvedUses: Labor induction/augmentation; control of postpartum bleeding (IV/IM).
oxytocin.offLabelNote: Intimacy/mood/libido use via sublingual/nasal compounds is off-label.

tadalafil.compounding: Commercial product available; compounded troches/combinations used.

sildenafil.compounding: Commercial product available; compounded troches/combinations used.

thymosin-alpha-1.status: NOT FDA-approved in the US; approved in many countries as Zadaxin.
thymosin-alpha-1.compounding: Under FDA peptide bulk-substance review; restricted US compounding. Verify current status.
approvedUses: Outside the US: chronic hepatitis B/C, immune adjunct (Zadaxin).
thymosin-alpha-1.offLabelNote: All US clinical use is investigational/off-label.

glutathione.status: NOT FDA-approved as a drug; injectable forms compounded; oral as supplement.
glutathione.compounding: IV/IM glutathione compounded via 503A/503B. FDA has warned against certain skin-whitening injectable glutathione products.
glutathione.offLabelNote: Antioxidant/detox/skin use is off-label/supplement; IV skin-whitening claims unapproved.
references: FDA warnings on injectable skin-whitening products

low-dose-naltrexone.status: Naltrexone is FDA-approved at 50 mg (alcohol/opioid dependence); LDN (1.5–4.5 mg) is off-label compounded.
low-dose-naltrexone.compounding: Low doses compounded via 503A from approved naltrexone.
approvedUses: Alcohol use disorder; opioid use disorder (at 50 mg).
low-dose-naltrexone.offLabelNote: Immune/inflammatory/pain use at low dose is entirely off-label.

finasteride.compounding: Commercial tablets available; compounded topical formulations used off-label.
finasteride.offLabelNote: Topical finasteride and use in women are off-label; pregnancy category concerns.

oral-minoxidil.status: Minoxidil is FDA-approved as oral antihypertensive (high dose) and topical for hair (OTC); low-dose ORAL for hair is off-label.
oral-minoxidil.compounding: Low-dose oral compounded from approved minoxidil; topical is OTC.
oral-minoxidil.offLabelNote: Low-dose oral minoxidil for hair loss is off-label (well-supported in dermatology literature).

ghk-cu.status: NOT an FDA-approved drug; widely used as a topical cosmeceutical.
ghk-cu.compounding: Topical cosmetic ingredient; injectable forms compounded off-label and under peptide scrutiny.
ghk-cu.offLabelNote: Topical cosmetic use; injectable use is investigational/off-label.

tretinoin.compounding: Commercial products available; compounded blends (with niacinamide, hydroquinone, azelaic acid) used.
approvedUses: Acne vulgaris; mitigation of fine wrinkles/photoaging (tretinoin 0.05% emollient).
tretinoin.offLabelNote: Some compounded combination concentrations are off-label.
